eventLoop.title: Event Loop Visualizer
eventLoop.tooltip.callStack:
**Call Stack**
- Tracks function execution in **LIFO** (Last In, First Out) order
- When a function is called, it's **pushed** onto the stack
- When it returns, it's **popped** off
- JavaScript is **single-threaded** — only one function runs at a time
- If the stack is empty, the event loop can pick the next task
eventLoop.tooltip.webApis:
**Web APIs**
- Browser-provided interfaces that run **outside** the JS engine
- Examples: **setTimeout**, **fetch**, **DOM events**, **XMLHttpRequest**
- When called from the Call Stack, the browser handles them in the **background**
- Once complete, the callback is moved to the **Task Queue** or **Microtask Queue**
eventLoop.tooltip.taskQueue:
**Task Queue (Macrotasks)**
- Also called the **Callback Queue** or **Macrotask Queue**
- Holds callbacks from:
  - **setTimeout** / **setInterval**
  - **DOM events** (click, load, etc.)
  - **I/O operations**
- Event loop picks **one task at a time**
- Only runs after the Call Stack is empty **and** all microtasks are drained
eventLoop.tooltip.microtaskQueue:
**Microtask Queue**
- Has **higher priority** than the Task Queue
- Holds callbacks from:
  - **Promise.then()** / **.catch()** / **.finally()**
  - **async/await** continuations
  - **queueMicrotask()**
- **All** microtasks are drained before the next macrotask
- This is why Promises resolve **before** setTimeout callbacks
eventLoop.tooltip.eventLoop:
**Event Loop Cycle**
- **Step 1:** Execute all synchronous code on the Call Stack
- **Step 2:** Drain **all** microtasks from the Microtask Queue
- **Step 3:** Pick **one** task from the Task Queue
- **Step 4:** Rerender the UI if needed, then repeat from Step 2

**Key rule:** Microtasks always run before the next macrotask
eventLoop.howItWorks.title: How It Works
eventLoop.howItWorks.callStack.label: 1. Call Stack
eventLoop.howItWorks.callStack.descripti: Synchronous code executes here, one frame at a time (LIFO).
eventLoop.howItWorks.webApis.label: 2. Web APIs
eventLoop.howItWorks.webApis.description: setTimeout, fetch, etc. register callbacks externally.
eventLoop.howItWorks.taskQueue.label: 3. Task Queue
eventLoop.howItWorks.taskQueue.descripti: Callbacks from setTimeout and setInterval wait here.
eventLoop.howItWorks.microtaskQueue.desc: Promise .then() callbacks and async/await continuations go here. Microtasks always run before the next task.
eventLoop.howItWorks.asyncAwait.label: 5. async/await
eventLoop.howItWorks.asyncAwait.descript: Code before await runs synchronously. The await pauses the function, scheduling its continuation as a microtask.
eventLoop.arrowLegend.title: Arrow Legend
eventLoop.arrowLegend.register.name: Register
eventLoop.arrowLegend.register.descripti: Call Stack sends async operations (setTimeout, fetch) to Web APIs
eventLoop.arrowLegend.timerDone.name: Timer done → Task
eventLoop.arrowLegend.timerDone.descript: setTimeout/setInterval completes in Web API and moves callback to Task Queue
eventLoop.arrowLegend.taskCb.name: Task cb
eventLoop.arrowLegend.taskCb.description: Event loop picks a task from Task Queue and pushes it onto Call Stack
eventLoop.arrowLegend.resolve.name: Resolve
eventLoop.arrowLegend.resolve.descriptio: Promise resolution, await, or fetch completion schedules a callback into Microtask Queue
eventLoop.arrowLegend.microCb.name: Micro cb
eventLoop.arrowLegend.microCb.descriptio: Event loop drains all microtasks onto Call Stack before the next task
eventLoop.phases.title: Event Loop
eventLoop.phases.evaluateScript.name: Evaluate Script
eventLoop.phases.evaluateScript.descript: Execute synchronously until call stack is empty
eventLoop.phases.runTask.name: Run a Task
eventLoop.phases.runTask.description: Dequeue and execute the oldest task
eventLoop.phases.runMicrotasks.name: Run all Microtasks
eventLoop.phases.runMicrotasks.descripti: Execute microtasks until the queue is empty
eventLoop.phases.rerender.name: Rerender
eventLoop.phases.rerender.description: Update the UI, then loop back to step 2
renderingPipeline.title: Browser Rendering Pipeline
renderingPipeline.subtitle: Visualize how browsers transform HTML, CSS, and JavaScript into pixels on screen
renderingPipeline.stages.parsing.name: HTML Parsing
renderingPipeline.stages.parsing.descrip: The browser parses HTML and builds the Document Object Model (DOM) tree. Each HTML element becomes a node in the tree.
renderingPipeline.stages.cssom.name: CSSOM Construction
renderingPipeline.stages.cssom.descripti: CSS is parsed and the CSS Object Model (CSSOM) tree is built. Styles are computed and inheritance is resolved.
renderingPipeline.stages.renderTree.name: Render Tree Construction
renderingPipeline.stages.renderTree.desc: DOM and CSSOM are combined to create the Render Tree. Only visible elements are included (display:none excluded).
renderingPipeline.stages.layout.name: Layout (Reflow)
renderingPipeline.stages.layout.descript: The browser calculates the exact position and size of each element. Also called 'reflow' - this is expensive!
renderingPipeline.stages.paint.name: Paint
renderingPipeline.stages.paint.descripti: The browser fills in pixels - drawing text, colors, images, borders, shadows. Creates paint records.
renderingPipeline.stages.compositing.des: Layers are combined in the correct order. GPU-accelerated transforms and opacity happen here - the fastest path!
flexbox.title: Flexbox Playground
grid.title: CSS Grid Playground
positioning.title: CSS Positioning
boxModel.title: Box Model Visualizer
positioning.info.static.title: Static (Default)
positioning.info.static.description: Elements are positioned according to normal document flow. Top, right, bottom, left, and z-index have no effect.
positioning.info.relative.title: Relative
positioning.info.relative.description: Element is positioned relative to its normal position. Offsets move it from where it would normally be, but its original space is preserved.
positioning.info.absolute.title: Absolute
positioning.info.absolute.description: Element is positioned relative to its nearest positioned ancestor (or the viewport if none exists). Removed from normal document flow.
positioning.info.fixed.title: Fixed
positioning.info.fixed.description: Element is positioned relative to the viewport. It stays in the same place even when scrolling. Removed from normal document flow.
positioning.info.sticky.title: Sticky
positioning.info.sticky.description: Element is positioned based on scroll position. It toggles between relative and fixed depending on scroll position.
